author: Dr. Anna Reyes (PGY-2, on the medicine service) / Dr. Jennifer Beckman (Medicine attending)
content:
DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — 4/2/2027, 10:00 AM (Hospital Day 47, Floor day 21)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Location: 5N bed 14
Interpreter: Vietnamese phone interpreter used.

24-HOUR EVENTS:
Quiet night. Slept ~6 hours.
- PT yesterday: ambulated 200 feet with rolling walker without assist; transferred chair-to-bed independently. Significant improvement.
- OT yesterday: lower-body dressing now requires only minimal supervision. Practiced kitchen ADLs with adaptive equipment.
- Tolerating regular diet, ~85% intake. Ensure BID continues.
- Weight today 56.0 kg (up from nadir 51.8 kg HD 25, but still down 6 kg from admission 62 kg).
- No bleeding, no fevers, no respiratory symptoms.

UPDATE ON DISCHARGE PLANNING:
The acute rehab placement (Bayview Acute Rehab) that was anticipated for early April has been DELAYED — they no longer have a bed available until 4/8 at the earliest (potentially 4/12). Case management is exploring alternative SAR/SNF rehab facilities to avoid further inpatient days. Two SNF options identified:
- FutureCare Charles Village (10 min from her son's home, Vietnamese interpreter on staff some days): bed available 4/12.
- Genesis Long-Term & Rehabilitation (further, no Vietnamese-speaking staff): bed available 4/5.

Family strongly prefers FutureCare Charles Village. Acceptable plan: wait for FutureCare bed (4/12) since clinically stable.

SYSTEMS:

NEURO: A+Ox3. Good engagement. Mood stable.

RESP: RA, SpO2 97%. No issues.

CV: BP 138/80 (mildly higher; reviewing); HR 82 sinus. No edema. Right LE: residual mild swelling (~ trace), no tenderness.

GI: Regular diet tolerated. No abdominal pain. Bowel movements regular. No nausea.

RENAL: Cr 1.3 (baseline). UOP good.

HEME: Apixaban 5 mg BID day 31. Hgb 10.2 (improving). No bleeding events. Plt 268.

MSK / FUNCTIONAL: Continued improvement. Independent ambulation with rolling walker on flat surfaces; needs supervision on uneven surfaces. Can climb a half-flight of stairs with handrail + 1-person standby. Major remaining functional gap: stair-climbing for her son's home (3rd-floor walk-up that the family lives in — patient lives with them on the 2nd floor up a half-flight of stairs from the building entrance).

PROBLEMS / PLAN (unchanged from recent days):
#1 Recovery from severe acute biliary pancreatitis + ARDS + DVT — clinically resolved; functionally still recovering.
#2 RLE DVT — on apixaban; uneventful.
#3 Cholelithiasis — General Surgery already counseled regarding outpatient cholecystectomy ~6-8 weeks post-rehab discharge. Will not interrupt rehabilitation.
#4 ICU-acquired weakness, improving with active rehabilitation.
#5 HTN — slight uptick today; will recheck this PM. Continue lisinopril 5 mg.
#6 HLD — continue atorvastatin.
#7 LTBI, anemia, CKD III — known, monitored, stable.

DISCHARGE TIMELINE: Pending SNF acceptance — likely 4/12 to FutureCare Charles Village. Until then, continue current management and rehabilitation.

Family meeting today canceled (son working late); rescheduled to 4/4. Discharge teaching to be initiated 1 week before anticipated discharge.

Documented at the patient's bedside this morning.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Anna Reyes, MD (PGY-2, Internal Medicine)
Dr. Jennifer Beckman, MD (Attending)
Date: 4/2/2027
